# flashblade_nfs_export_policy_rule (Resource)

Manages a rule within a FlashBlade NFS export policy.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "flashblade_nfs_export_policy_rule" "example" {
  policy_name = "terraform-nfs-policy"

  client     = "10.0.0.0/8"
  permission = "rw"
  access     = "root-squash"
  security   = ["sys"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `policy_name` (String) The name of the NFS export policy this rule belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `access` (String) The access control for NFS clients (e.g. 'root-squash', 'no-root-squash', 'all-squash').
- `anongid` (Number) The GID to use for anonymous (squashed) users.
- `anonuid` (Number) The UID to use for anonymous (squashed) users.
- `atime` (Boolean) If true, access time updates are enabled.
- `client` (String) A pattern matching the clients to which this rule applies (e.g. '*', '10.0.0.0/8').
- `fileid_32bit` (Boolean) If true, use 32-bit file IDs.
- `permission` (String) The read/write permission for matching clients (e.g. 'rw', 'ro').
- `required_transport_security` (String) Required transport security for this rule (e.g. 'krb5', 'krb5i', 'krb5p').
- `secure` (Boolean) If true, require clients to use a privileged port.
- `security` (List of String) Security flavors to enforce for this rule.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the NFS export policy rule (server-assigned UUID).
- `index` (Number) The server-assigned ordering index for this rule within the policy. Used for import.
- `name` (String) The server-assigned rule identifier. Used internally for PATCH/DELETE API calls.
- `policy_version` (String) The version of the parent policy at the time this rule was last read.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using composite ID: policy_name/rule_index
terraform import flashblade_nfs_export_policy_rule.example my-nfs-policy/0
```
